rationale: Verified the round-2 delta at 389fcc41 is exactly the described 2-file, seam-transparent reroute. internal/lifecycle/gitquery.go adds IsAncestor(workdir, ancestor, descendant string) (bool, error) and BranchExists(name string) bool as thin pass-throughs to gitutil.IsAncestor/gitutil.BranchExists — signatures match the underlying gitutil functions exactly (confirmed against internal/gitutil/gitops.go). internal/approve/impl.go now sets implIsAncestorFn = lifecycle.IsAncestor and implBranchExistsFn = lifecycle.BranchExists, and the internal/gitutil import line is removed entirely; `go list -f '{{.Imports}}' ./internal/approve` confirms no gitutil import remains. The updated comments accurately describe the reroute (e.g. 'via lifecycle's own internal seam') and do not falsely claim a direct gitutil import — they correctly attribute the underlying git plumbing to gitutil while noting the access is now indirect. go build ./... is clean and TestEnforcementHasNoGitLeaks passes. Seam names/types/injection points are unchanged so all AC tests are unaffected; my round-1 grounding APPROVE (with its one non-blocking minor line-drift note) holds unchanged since this delta touches only the git-access boundary, not gate behavior.
